eulogy:
JOHN ‘KUKA’ NYIKULI SHIKANGA EULOGY



John ‘Kuka’ Nyikuli Shikanga was born in 8th August 1942 in Samitsi Village to Thomas Shikanga Lunani and Selina Kharamwa Shikanga (both late). He schooled at Samitsi Primary school and Shamberere Intermediate School exiting in 1959.

He later proceeded to Kampala Uganda in 1960 where he worked at the Goan Institute as a store keeper and club controller until 1962. He later moved to Nairobi, to join Reckitt & Colman as a Van Salesman. He got married to the late Hellen Mulagoli in 1964 under customary law. They were blessed with one son, Willingstone Nyikuli Lunani. Upon Mama Hellen’s demise in 2011, he married Monicah Kanyua in 2015. 

John studied with Jehovah’s Witnesses and was baptized on 19th February 1967 in Nairobi. He later moved to Nakuru joining the Nakuru East (English) Congregation in 1970. He was privileged to serve as an Elder in several Congregations, the last being at Butali Congregation. 

Many who knew him acknowledge his mastery of Bible teachings that enabled several persons to be drawn to serve Jehovah while he was travelling across the West region of Kenya as a Salesman for Cadbury Schweppes (1970 -1975) and BRITAM (1975 -2005). In point and fact he was a father to many Christian brothers.

In 2019 he started showing signs of poor health, and visited several hospitals in Nakuru, Kisumu, Kakamega and later Webuye towns. On 14th January 2026 his Family Doctor (Maxi Care Hospital in Webuye) diagnosed a mild stroke that had affected his prefrontal cortex and age related atrophy. Later on 12th February 2026 he was diagnosed with liver related ailment that brought him down on 1st March 2026.

John, known to many as ‘Kuka’ is survived by his wife, Monicah Kanyua, son, Willingstone Lunani Nyikuli, Daughter-in-law Margaret Aloya Lunani, Grandson Samuel Nyikuli Lunani, Granddaughter Hellen Ayuma Lunani and great granddaughter Amber Thorne Lunani. Brother to Tamara, Trufena, Rasoa, late Hezron, Josiah, Henry, Joan, Jane, Irene, late Ezekiel, Mboya, late Nyongesa, late Musengeli, Levi, Zacharia, Luile. Basanji to late Mzee David Otemba and Mama Gladys Otemba.

As you know, ‘Kuka’ has been endlessly generous with our community through his endeavors and with all of us who have been fortunate enough to call ourselves his family and friends. ‘Kuka’ is gone and will be greatly missed.



Thank you.
tribute:
TRIBUTE TO MY FATHER ‘KUKA’

I am humbled on this day when paying tribute to my late Father and Brother Mzee John Nyikuli Shikanga. In my heart I refer to him as ‘John Best’ Why? Because he endeavored to give me the best things in life:



He ensured that I got the best formal education in prestigious Nursery, Primary, Secondary and College Institutions available

The best gift ‘John Best’ gave me was Christian Education in Jehovah’s organization. He patiently waited for me to dedicate my life to Jehovah and get baptized and travelled to Nairobi to Witness it. On paper I was born alone however in reality I find myself surrounded by many genuine brothers and sisters - corner to corner, world over courtesy of him.

He inculcated in me a spirit of fairness, reasonableness and perseverance when times are hard for which you can see and I say thank you.

As we lay him to rest today, I boldly take over the mantle from him so as to ensure that his dreams and wishes for the family are upheld. My sincere acknowledgements to Mama Monica for taking care of ‘John Best’ in his last 10yrs of life. 

Special thanks to our brothers and sisters at Butali and the surrounding Congregations. May Jehovah bless the good work of your hands – Asante sana!



‘’ Kusudi la Yehova Litatimizwa” Isa 46; 11 | Yoh 11:25
